lessons.title: Unit 1: The pancreas and body systems
lessons.sourceTitle:
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � pancreas
MedlinePlus � Digestive System
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from NCI and MedlinePlus in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: The pancreas is an organ. An organ is a body part made of tissues, and a tissue is made of many cells. The pancreas is a gland because it makes useful substances for the body. It helps the digestive system by sending enzymes through a duct to help break down food. It also helps the endocrine system by making hormones, which are chemical messages that travel in the blood. So the pancreas has two big jobs: helping digest food and helping control blood sugar.
lessons.prompt: This course now starts with very basic body words. Read the passage, then answer three questions.

choices: An organ that helps with digestion and blood sugar
It carries fluid through a small tube
It pumps blood like the heart
It stores thoughts in the brain

questions.prompt: Why is the pancreas called both digestive and endocrine?
choices: Because it helps digest food and also makes hormones
Because it only makes skin cells
Because it does no job in the body

lessons.title: Unit 2: Inside a cell
lessons.sourceTitle:
MedlinePlus Genetics � What is a cell?
MedlinePlus Genetics � What is DNA?
MedlinePlus Genetics � What is a chromosome?
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from MedlinePlus Genetics in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: A cell has parts with different jobs. The cell membrane is the outside layer that helps protect the cell. Inside is the cytoplasm, a jelly-like material where many cell jobs happen. The nucleus is the part that holds DNA. DNA contains genes, and genes are instructions for making proteins. Proteins help cells work. Cells also use receptors to receive a signal from outside the cell. When a cell gets the right signal, it may grow or divide. Cell division is how one cell becomes two cells, and mitosis is one common kind of cell division.
lessons.prompt: Now move inside the cell and learn the words that appear in basic biology.

questions.prompt: Where is DNA found in this lesson?

lessons.title: Unit 3: Cancer, tumors, and spread
lessons.sourceTitle:
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � cancer
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � tumor
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � metastasis
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from NCI in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: Cancer is a disease in which cells grow too much and do not follow normal rules. A tumor is a mass, or lump, made of abnormal cells. Some tumors are benign, which means they are not cancer and are not expected to spread. Other tumors are malignant, which means they are cancer and can invade nearby tissue. If cancer cells spread far away and grow in a new place, that is called metastasis. Doctors may also use the word lesion for an area of abnormal tissue seen on a scan or in the body.
lessons.prompt: These are the first cancer words a beginner should know.

choices: A type of white blood cell
Cancerous and able to invade or spread
Cancer spreading to another part of the body

lessons.title: Unit 4: DNA changes and inheritance
lessons.sourceTitle:
MedlinePlus Genetics � What is DNA?
MedlinePlus Genetics � What is a gene?
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � mutation
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from MedlinePlus Genetics and NCI in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: Genes are written in DNA, and DNA can change. A mutation is a change in DNA. Some genetic changes are inherited, which means they are passed from parent to child. Other changes happen later during life. DNA can also be damaged by mistakes or by harmful exposures. Cells have repair systems that try to fix DNA damage. When repair does not work well, more genetic changes can build up over time.
lessons.prompt: This unit keeps genetics simple and focuses on the words beginners see first.

choices: Receiving genes or traits from parents
Because cells try to fix DNA damage
Because it changes bones into muscles
Because it stops all disease forever
Because it is the same as metastasis

lessons.title: Unit 5: Symptoms and body changes
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from MedlinePlus in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: A disease or disorder is a health problem that changes how the body works. Some diseases are caused by infection, while others are not. Inflammation is a body response to injury or infection and can lead to pain and swelling. Jaundice means yellowing of the skin or eyes. Weight loss can also be a sign that the body is under stress or not getting enough nutrition. These words do not name one single disease, but they help people describe what is happening in the body.
lessons.prompt: A new learner also needs common disease words and symptom words.

choices: A body response to injury or infection
Yellowing of the skin or eyes
Any operation done by a doctor

lessons.title: Unit 6: Tests and finding a diagnosis
lessons.sourceTitle:
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � biopsy
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � CT scan
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � MRI
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from NCI in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: Doctors use tests to learn what is happening in the body. A scan is a picture made with a machine, and imaging is the general word for making those pictures. A blood test uses a blood sample. A biopsy uses a small tissue or cell sample. Salivary testing means testing saliva, or spit, for useful information. All of these tools can help with diagnosis, which means finding out what disease or condition a person has.
lessons.prompt: This unit explains the very basic words used when doctors look for answers.

choices: A small tissue or cell sample taken for testing
A way to stop all pain right away
Finding out what disease or condition a person has
Turning a benign tumor into a malignant one
Making pictures of the inside of the body
Writing down genes with a pencil
A hormone made by the pancreas

lessons.title: Unit 7: Ways doctors treat disease
lessons.sourceTitle:
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � treatment
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � chemotherapy
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � radiation therapy
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from NCI in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: Treatment is care used to help a disease or condition. Surgery is treatment done with an operation. Medicine is a drug used to help the body. Chemotherapy is a kind of medicine used to kill cancer cells or slow their growth. Radiation is high-energy treatment used against some cancers. Therapy is a broad word for treatment, and recovery means getting better after illness or treatment. Different people may need different treatments depending on the disease and how far it has gone.
lessons.prompt: After diagnosis comes treatment. This unit keeps those treatment words simple.

choices: Drug treatment used to kill cancer cells or slow their growth
Getting better after illness or treatment
A receptor on the cell membrane
A scan of the pancreas only

lessons.title: Unit 8: Things that can raise risk
lessons.sourceTitle:
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � risk factor
NCI Dictionary of Cancer Terms � smoking
MedlinePlus � Family History
lessons.sourceCredit: Adapted from NCI and MedlinePlus in simpler language.
lessons.readingText: A risk factor is something that raises the chance of getting a disease. A risk factor does not guarantee that someone will get sick, but it can make the chance higher. Smoking is one example of a risk factor for many diseases. Diet, age, and family history can matter too. Learning about risk factors is part of learning about health, because risk is about chances and patterns, not certainty.
lessons.prompt: The last beginner unit explains risk factors using everyday words.

choices: Something that raises the chance of disease
A guarantee that disease will happen
A treatment that always cures cancer
A part of the cell membrane

questions.prompt: Which choice is listed as a risk factor in this unit?

choices: It can help show patterns of disease in a family
It turns all benign tumors malignant
It is the same thing as a biopsy
It means a person must already be sick
